name: Devnw
description_short: DevNW is a nonprofit community development organization offering free financial counseling, affordable housing programs, and access to funds across the Pacific Northwest.
description_long:
DevNW is a nonprofit organization founded in 2019 through the merger of two established nonprofits: Neighborhood Economic Development Corporation (NEDCO) and Willamette Neighborhood Housing Services (WNHS). Together, these organizations brought 60 years of combined experience in community development. The organization operates across the Northwest with a mission to grow vibrant communities by empowering individuals, families, small businesses, and neighborhoods through practical, goal-oriented support.

DevNW offers a comprehensive suite of financial and housing services organized into four main categories: Financial Wellbeing (one-on-one financial counseling and educational classes), Affordable Homes (rental and homeownership programs), Access to Funds (matched savings accounts, down payment assistance, and consumer/small business loans), and Thriving Communities (neighborhood revitalization and economic development). All services are designed with the philosophy that success looks different for everyone and that wellbeing encompasses finances, housing, and community health.

What distinguishes DevNW is its integrated approach combining housing development, financial counseling, and community engagement under one organization. Rather than operating as a single-service provider, they address interconnected barriers to economic mobility through real estate development, lending products, education, and advocacy for policy change. The organization emphasizes local ownership and hands-on support, with staff who live in the communities they serve.

DevNW appears well-positioned as a legitimate nonprofit with substantial community roots and infrastructure. However, the website provides limited detail on loan terms, interest rates, counselor certifications (though free-help category typically implies HUD or NFCC certification), or specific program eligibility requirements. Potential clients should contact directly for detailed program specifics and availability.
pros: Established nonprofit with 60 years of combined organizational history through predecessor organizations
Integrated services model addressing housing, financial counseling, lending, and community development simultaneously
Offers free financial counseling and classes as part of Financial Wellbeing program
Provides down payment assistance and matched savings accounts for homebuyers
Operates affordable housing development as both rental and homeownership pathways
Staff have personal stake in communities they serve (stated to live in service areas)
Offers small business and consumer loans in addition to individual financial counseling
cons: Website lacks specific information on loan terms, APRs, or repayment structures for lending products
No details provided on counselor certifications (NFCC, HUD approval status not mentioned)
Geographic service area limited to 'Northwest' with no specific states or service boundaries clearly defined
Limited information on eligibility requirements or income thresholds for programs
No pricing or fee structure disclosed for any counseling or lending services
best_for: Pacific Northwest residents seeking free financial counseling and education combined with affordable housing access
First-time homebuyers looking for down payment assistance and matched savings programs
Small business owners in the Northwest seeking community-based lending and support
Individuals and families in underserved neighborhoods seeking integrated economic development support
diagnosis: DevNW is best for Pacific Northwest residents seeking comprehensive, free financial counseling integrated with affordable housing and lending products from an established nonprofit with deep community roots. The main caveat is that the website provides limited detail on specific program eligibility, loan terms, certifications, and geographic service boundaries—prospective clients must contact directly for detailed program information and availability.
services: Financial wellbeing classes and educational workshops
Homeownership pathway programs and home maintenance support
Community engagement and neighborhood revitalization services
Real estate development and affordable housing construction
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 421 High Street, Suite 110, Oregon City, OR 97045
